A first start Warwick Farm win by Switzerland’s brother St Gotthard and Black Caviar’s daughter Persian Caviar at Sandown were highlight victories on Wednesday. At Doomben, jockey Ben Thompson dominated the card, winning five of the first six races on the card. (Switzerland's Brother, Black Caviar's Daughter Win; Thompson Dominates Doomben Card)

Another bumper catalogue for Inglis Digital concluded on Wednesday, and was topped by winning 5-year-old mare Flying Fizz, who was bought by Corumbene Stud for $175,000. (Flying Fizz Sells for $175K to Top Inglis Digital Sale)

Woodside Park Stud have dropped all their fees across the board in 2026 providing value for Victorian breeders who are feeling the pinch of the current economic climate. Dual Group 1 winner Celestial Legend leads the roster of six stallions at $24,200 including GST. (Woodside Park Stud Drops Fees for 2026; Celestial Legend Leads Roster)

The first day of the Tattersalls Craven Breeze-Up Sale came backwards in almost all metrics, with only the median rising by 6%. A Palace Pier from dual Group 1 winner Majmu, an Australian bred daughter of Redoute’s Choice, topped the day at 450,000 gns. (Tattersalls Craven Breeze-Up Day 1 Sees Mixed Results; Palace Pier Colt Tops at 450,000 gns)

Day 1 of Ocala’s Spring 2-Year-Olds in Training Sale led with a colt by Epicenter lit up the ring at US$1.95 million on the first day of the four-day auction. The session average was up over 20% on last year with the day grossing over US$24.5 million. (Epicenter Colt Brings $1.95M on Opening Day of OBS Spring Sale; Average Up 20%)

Last year's G1 Champagne Stakes winner Nepotism has been retired.
